description: Multi-tenant React 18 + Vite frontend. Vanilla CSS, no TypeScript, no React Query, no MSW. The mock in lib/api/mock/ is the spec of the real backend.

features.name: resolve_session_me_endpoint
features.title: Remove getCurrentUser and the ApiSessionProvider branch from SessionProvider
features.description: Mirror of back Phase 3 feature 3 (Option B). The backend does not expose GET /me and there is no users table; effective identity is derived by the front from the admin bearer token (super-admin) or from the GHL SSO context (agency_user). sessionApi.getCurrentUser, the ApiSessionProvider component and the branch that selected it are removed; the root SessionProvider now always renders GhlMvpSessionProvider. The .session-fallback / .session-fallback.loading rules are kept because they are consumed by the surviving provider's 'Connecting GoHighLevel location...' screen (documented deviation from back spike §6.3 — Option A for CSS).

features.name: frontend_admin_auth_lockstep
features.title: Attach real authorization in apiRequest for /v1/admin/*
features.description: Mirror of backend feature 5. The frontend already points to real routes, but src/lib/api/client.js does not attach Authorization and the backend rejects /v1/admin/* except in testing. This feature implements the frontend side of the live contract: agency-scoped token from the GoHighLevel session and explicit local super-admin mode without embedding production secrets into the bundle.
acceptance: src/lib/api/client.js attaches Authorization: Bearer <token> when the authorized session exposes it
The agency-scoped token returned by /v1/sessions/gohighlevel/session is stored and cleared in a controlled way in src/features/session/
Admin-direct mode supports an explicit local super-admin token without recommending VITE_ADMIN_API_TOKEN in production
/v1/admin/* happy-path calls do not return 401/503 with ADMIN_API_DISABLE_AUTH_FOR_TESTING=false
No real secrets hardcoded in the bundle or in .env.example
tests/support/mock-backend.js and Playwright cover, where applicable, the presence/absence of Authorization
npm run lint finishes green
npm run build finishes green
npm run test:smoke finishes green
verification: Manual: real backend with ADMIN_API_DISABLE_AUTH_FOR_TESTING=false, test admin-direct and GHL session; DevTools without 401/503 on /v1/admin/*

features.name: fix_frontend_backend_payload_contract
features.title: Align Sources, Brand and Automation with strict Pydantic payloads
features.description: Mirror of backend feature 6. Routes and verbs match, but several screens send fields the backend rejects with extra='forbid'. This feature fixes the payloads sent by Admin Sources, Brand and Automation, moves Platforms to the correct owner (/defaults) or documents it, and updates stale mocks/docs.
acceptance: Sources sends name and status, not source_name or source_status
Editing Sources uses PUT /v1/admin/agencies/{agency_id}/sources/{ingestion_source_id}; create still uses POST /sources
Brand sends only primary_color, secondary_color, logo_position, logo_object_key, intro_logo_object_key and font_family
Brand reads font_family from the backend; does not send font, tagline, watermark_enabled, outro_enabled, outro_headline or outro_sub to /brand
Automation sends only approval_required, publish_window_start, publish_window_end, publish_days and trigger_on_status
Automation does not send publish_mode, platforms, review_window_enabled, review_window_hours, quiet_hours_enabled, skip_weekends, auto_captions, regen_on_update or review_emails to /automation
Platforms are persisted from /defaults or it is documented that the UI does not save them from Automation
tests/support/mock-backend.js reflects the canonical contract and does not hide 422s that the real backend would produce
DOCS.md and .env.example stop asserting that everything is mocked if the live client is the real source

features.name: wire_automation_publish_window_to_ghl_schedule

features.name: approve_button_label_and_drop_publish_stub
features.title: Rename Approve to 'Approve & Publish' and remove the coming-soon Publish button
features.description: The backend already publishes on receiving POST /approve (regenerate_reel.py:255 forces approval_required=False and enqueues the reel_publish job that fires MEDIA PUBLISH to the connected networks via GoHighLevel). In the reel editor 'Approve' (which approves+publishes) and a disabled 'Publish' with tooltip 'Manual publishing on the roadmap' coexist. That coexistence is confusing: users think Approve only approves and Publish is the next step, when in reality Approve already does both. Rename to 'Approve & Publish' (and the corresponding aria-label) and remove the else branch with the coming-soon button in ReelEditor.jsx EditorHeader.
acceptance: src/features/reels/editor/ReelEditor.jsx shows label 'Approve & Publish' (not just 'Approve') in the header primary button when publishStatus === 'needs-approval'
The disabled coming-soon Publish button (else branch of the canApproveOrReject ternary) is removed; when publishStatus !== 'needs-approval' the header shows no publish button
The old tooltip 'Manual publishing from the editor is on the roadmap...' no longer appears in the bundle (grep -rn 'Manual publishing from the editor' src/ returns 0 hits)
The existing smoke tests that check approve remain green (adjust the text selector if they used literally 'Approve')

features.name: fix_mojibake_in_source_files
features.title: Repair mojibake (UTF-8 double-encoded) in frontend JSX/JS files
features.description: Multiple frontend files contain doubly-encoded UTF-8 sequences (latin1↔utf-8). Characters like — … ' · € ≤ → ⌘ ✓ appear as 'â€—', 'â€¦', 'â€™', 'Â·', 'â‚¬', 'â‰¤', 'â†’', 'âŒ˜', 'âœ”' both in rendered strings (search placeholder, variable samples, keyboard shortcuts, 'Loading…', '·' separators in headers, '—' editor fallback) and in JSDoc comments. The user sees them in the UI. Inventory in `progress/explore_mojibake_inventory.md`: 13 files (11 in src/, 2 in tests/), 38 occurrences. Fix: apply the inversion bytes.encode('latin-1').decode('utf-8') to the mojibake sequences using explicit pair replacements so already-correct UTF-8 characters are not corrupted (some files may have a mix).
acceptance: grep -rn -P 'â€|Â·|â‚¬|â‰¤|â†|âŒ˜|âœ' src/ tests/ returns 0 hits after the fix
The rendered strings the user saw corrupted now show the correct character: '—' (em dash), '…' (ellipsis), '·' (middle dot), '€' (euro), '⌘' (command), '≤' (less or equal), '→' (right arrow)
src/app/Topbar.jsx shows placeholder 'Search reels, properties…' and the kbd hint '⌘K'
src/features/reels/editor/ReelEditor.jsx:331 shows '· {reel.address}' and the '—' fallbacks in Render/Workflow
src/app/providers/TenantProvider.jsx samples show '2-bed apartment · Cranford Court' and '€385,000'
Modified files remain valid UTF-8 (file --mime-encoding returns utf-8)
Reviewable diff: only mojibake characters change, no spurious changes in logic or indentation
npm run lint finishes green
npm run build finishes green
npm run test:smoke finishes green
verification: Manual: open the UI in a browser and check the topbar (placeholder + ⌘K) and reel editor (· separator in header, — fallbacks)

features.name: map_backend_publish_status_values
features.title: Map the real backend publish_status values (pending_review, pending_publish, skipped)
features.description: The backend returns `publish_status` with values the frontend does not recognize: 'pending_review' (when the reel is waiting for human approval, see publish_reel.py:225), 'pending_publish' (between the approve and the real publication, admin_reels_router.py:169) and 'skipped' (publish_reel.py:188,324). The frontend only maps 'awaiting_review' to 'needs-approval' (publishStatus.js:23), so when 'pending_review' arrives it falls through to the generic fallback, the UI does not recognize it as approvable and does NOT render the Approve buttons in ReelCard or in ReelEditor. Symptom reported by the user: in /reels pending reels show no button to publish. Fix: accept 'pending_review' as a synonym of 'awaiting_review' (keep 'awaiting_review' for compatibility), add a mapping of 'pending_publish' to a new 'publishing' badge and 'skipped' to a new 'skipped' badge in StatusBadge.jsx. Update the copy comment in AgencyConfigDrawer.jsx that still says 'awaiting_review'.
acceptance: src/features/reels/publishStatus.js maps 'pending_review' to 'needs-approval' (in addition to 'awaiting_review' for compatibility)
src/features/reels/publishStatus.js maps 'pending_publish' to 'publishing' and 'skipped' to 'skipped'
The JSDoc comment at the top of publishStatus.js reflects the values the backend returns today (pending_review instead of awaiting_review, also mentioning pending_publish and skipped)
src/shared/StatusBadge.jsx adds entries 'publishing' (cls 'info', label 'Publishing…') and 'skipped' (cls '', label 'Skipped')
src/features/admin/AgencyConfigDrawer.jsx:810 (or wherever the copy mentions 'awaiting_review') uses 'pending_review' to reflect what the user sees in the backend

tests/unit/publishStatus.unit.js exists testing: awaiting_review→needs-approval, pending_review→needs-approval, pending_publish→publishing, published→published, partial→published, approved→published, skipped→skipped, rejected→rejected, failed→failed, fallback with empty string→pending, fallback with unknown value→that value
ReelCard.jsx and ReelEditor.jsx keep showing Approve/Reject (no functional changes; the ternary still compares against 'needs-approval' after the map)
npm run lint finishes green
npm run build finishes green
npm run test:smoke finishes green
node --test tests/unit/publishStatus.unit.js finishes green
verification: Manual: with a reel in pending_review, check that in /reels the card shows the Approve/Reject buttons and the badge says 'Needs approval' (not raw 'pending_review')

features.name: templates_tab_agency_render_template_selection
features.title: New 'Templates' tab to select the agency's render template
features.description: Adds a new 'Templates' tab in the main front navigation that lets the agency user view and select the render template all reels of their agency will use. Wires up to the admin endpoints that already exist in the backend: GET /v1/admin/agencies/{agency_id}/render-templates (list of available packs + current_template_id) and PUT /v1/admin/agencies/{agency_id}/render-template (selects one). The screen shows a gallery with cards (display_name, description, preview_images[], 'Selected' badge on the active one) and a 'Use this template' button that fires the PUT. The exact shape is in modules/configuration/transport/http/render_templates_router.py in the backend. Real templates will be designed later — this feature only prepares the UI and API wiring; the content will be added by the backend in the catalog.

The button fires selectRenderTemplate and refreshes the list; toast/feedback for success or error following the pattern of the rest of the features

PUT returns { status:'saved', agency_id, render_template:{...selected:true} }

features.name: automation_scheduling_ui_hold_quiet_skip
features.title: Automation tab: move hold/quiet/skip to /automation and add time pickers for quiet hours
features.description: The Automation tab today persists hold/quiet/skip as opaque keys in /defaults.settings (automation.reviewWindowEnabled, automation.reviewWindowHours, automation.quietHoursEnabled, automation.skipWeekends). The back ignores those keys when computing the scheduleDate → immediate publication even if the user configures 'wait before publishing'. This feature moves the three controls to PUT /v1/admin/agencies/{id}/automation with the back's new fields (back feature 13): hold_window_seconds:int, quiet_hours_enabled:bool, skip_weekends:bool, and reuses publish_window_start/end (allowed hours, inverse of quiet) + publish_days. Adds <input type='time'> time pickers for quiet hours start/end (defaults 22:00 / 07:00). Hydrates state from /automation. Detailed plan in progress/plan_automation_scheduling_ui.md. Blocked by: backend feature 13 (without the schema deployed, PUTs return 422 because of extra='forbid').
acceptance: src/features/automation/AutoPublishDetails.jsx adds two <input type='time'> visible when the quiet hours toggle is active (defaults 22:00 and 07:00); keeps HoldPicker and skip weekends toggle

src/features/automation/hooks.js:buildAutomationBody maps holdWindowEnabled+holdWindowHours → hold_window_seconds, quietHoursEnabled → quiet_hours_enabled + publish_window_start/end (inverted), skipWeekends → skip_weekends + publish_days (mon-fri or mon-sun)

src/features/automation/AutomationConfig.jsx hydrates holdWindowEnabled/holdWindowHours/quietHours*/skipWeekends from /automation (not from /defaults.settings). The automation.* keys in the settings blob are no longer read
src/features/automation/useAutomationSave.js NO longer writes the automation.reviewWindowEnabled/.reviewWindowHours/.quietHoursEnabled/.skipWeekends keys to defaults.settings

tests/support/mock-backend.js: FORBIDDEN_KEYS['automation'] no longer includes quiet_hours_enabled or skip_weekends; the PUT /automation handler accepts and echoes back the new fields (hold_window_seconds, quiet_hours_enabled, skip_weekends) and returns them consistently in GET
tests/reel_approve_schedule.spec.js extended: 'hold 1h' case verifies the banner shows a scheduled_at ~1h in the future

tests/automation_scheduling.spec.js new: 3 cases (quiet hours at 23:00 → tomorrow 07:00; skip weekends Saturday 10:00 → Monday; hold 2h + skip weekends Friday 23:00 → Monday 07:00) with system clock mock

DOCS.md § Automation reflects the new contract (hold/quiet/skip travel in /automation; quiet_hours_* represented on the back as inverted publish_window; hours interpreted in agency.timezone local time)
grep -rn 'fetch(' src/features/automation returns 0 hits
npm run lint, npm run build, npm run test:smoke green
verification: npm run lint && npm run build

features.name: social_templates_ui_close_gaps
features.title: /social: complete exposed variables, validate unknown variables in mock and 422/limit test
features.description: The social-templates backend (/v1/admin/agencies/{id}/social-templates) accepts 16 canonical variables (see modules/configuration/domain/social_templates_variables.py:21-40) and rejects with 422 SOCIAL_TEMPLATE_UNKNOWN_VARIABLE any unknown one. The /social UI today only exposes 13 variables as clickable chips and the mock does not reject invalid variables, so the E2E tests never exercise the real error path. This feature: (1) adds neighborhood_tag, agent_email and property_url to STATIC_VARIABLES of TenantProvider; (2) makes the mock-backend validate unknown variables using the same canonical list and return 422 with the back's shape ({error:'SOCIAL_TEMPLATE_UNKNOWN_VARIABLE', details:{platform:[var,...]}}); (3) adds Playwright tests for (a) attempting to save {{bad_var}} → error banner with the code, (b) exceeding NETWORK_LIMITS[net] → counter in red and Save CTA still available if the UI does not block but document behavior; (4) extends the samples of the 3 new variables in STATIC_VARIABLES with realistic values.
acceptance: src/app/providers/TenantProvider.jsx:STATIC_VARIABLES includes neighborhood_tag (sample 'Cranford Court'), agent_email (sample 'sales@4pm.ie'), property_url (sample 'https://example.com/property/123')
After the change, useVariables() returns 16 entries and the order reflects the backend grouping (property fields, agent fields, links)

tests/support/mock-backend.js:handleSocialTemplates validates each description_template received in PUT with the same {{var}} regex as the backend and rejects with 422 + body {error:'SOCIAL_TEMPLATE_UNKNOWN_VARIABLE', details:{platform:['bad_var',...]}} if any variable is not in the canonical list of 16
An ALLOWED_SOCIAL_TEMPLATE_VARIABLES constant exists shared/exported between the provider (chips) and the mock (validation) so that one change covers both sites
tests/social_templates.spec.js (or new file) covers: saving {{nonexistent_var}} → error banner with 'SOCIAL_TEMPLATE_UNKNOWN_VARIABLE' visible
tests/social_templates.spec.js covers: typing text that exceeds NETWORK_LIMITS[instagram] (2200 chars) → counter <span> with class 'over' visible
tests/social_templates.spec.js covers: the 3 new variables (neighborhood_tag, agent_email, property_url) appear as clickable chips and on click are inserted into the textarea
grep -rn 'STATIC_VARIABLES' src returns only the provider (not duplicated in components)

npm run test:smoke finishes green

features.name: social_templates_ui_hashtags_and_title
features.title: /social: add hashtags editor and title_template per network, aligned with back
features.description: The backend already persists title_template (Text) and hashtags (Array<Text>) in agency_social_templates but the front's PUT only sends description_template and the UI does not allow editing those two fields. This feature (cross-repo with backend feature 18) extends the UI: for each network, in addition to the description textarea, add a title_template input (1 line, optional) and a chip editor for hashtags (input that parses spaces and commas, with '#' autocomplete). The PUT payload changes from {templates:{platform:string}} to {templates:{platform:{description_template, title_template, hashtags[]}}} if the back updates the contract; otherwise they remain as additional fields the back ignores until its counterpart ships.
features.depends_on: 4Reels-Backend feature 20 (extend social_templates payload with title_template + hashtags) deployed on :8001

src/features/social/api.js:saveSocialTemplates sends the new shape {templates:{platform:{description_template, title_template, hashtags}}} in the PUT (the backend accepts both this rich shape and the flat string for backward-compat)

src/features/social/hooks.js:useSocialTemplates hydrates the 3 fields from items[] of the GET response (each item has {platform, description_template, title_template, hashtags, created_at, updated_at}). DO NOT read from templates{} (legacy, description string only)
Hashtags are shown as chips with the same visual identity as the variable tag-chips

features.name: per_reel_description_override_ui
features.title: Reel editor: editable Descriptions tab, persists override per platform before approving
features.description: Today the rendered description (template + property variables) is stored in reels.publish_target_snapshot.descriptions_by_platform and is read-only from the editor. The user wants to be able to tweak the caption of ONE specific reel before approving it, without touching the agency's global template. This feature (cross-repo with backend feature 21) extends the ReelEditor 'Descriptions' tab so each network has an editable textarea hydrated with the snapshot, and on save persists via PATCH /v1/admin/reels/{reel_id}/descriptions (new back endpoint, feature 21). The worker's publish job, when executing, reads the override if it exists and uses it instead of re-rendering.
features.depends_on: 4Reels-Backend feature 21 (PATCH /v1/admin/reels/{reel_id}/descriptions + publish worker honors override) deployed on :8001
acceptance: src/features/reels/editor/ReelEditor.jsx Descriptions tab shows a panel per connected network with editable textarea, NETWORK_LIMITS[net] counter and Save button per network OR Save All

The textarea is hydrated with reel.publish_target_snapshot.descriptions_by_platform[platform] on load, and if reel.descriptions_override[platform] exists that override wins over the snapshot (precedence the back already implements)

Save fires PATCH /v1/admin/agencies/{agency_id}/reels/{site_id}/{source_property_id}/descriptions with body {descriptions_by_platform: {platform: text}}. Body {} clears the override to NULL (replace semantics of the back).
After Save, the editor reflects the override; if the agency later edits the global template in /social, reels with override do NOT change (the back already guarantees this in content_fingerprint)

If the reel is NOT in publish_status in ('needs-approval','pending_review','pending',''), textareas are read-only and an explanatory banner is shown; attempting PATCH from DevTools → 409 REEL_NOT_EDITABLE handled with feedback

If the user tries to save an override for a platform that is not in agency_reel_defaults.platforms, the back returns 422 PLATFORM_NOT_ENABLED; the UI already filters to 'connected networks' so this should only happen by manipulating DevTools — test that a toast/feedback with the code is shown

tests/support/mock-backend.js serves PATCH with shape {status:'saved', reel_id (or tuple), descriptions_override:{...}} and returns 409 REEL_NOT_EDITABLE / 422 PLATFORM_NOT_ENABLED depending on reel state
Playwright test: open a needs-approval reel, edit the instagram description, Save → mock receives PATCH with correct body
Playwright test: after override, reload the editor → the edited description persists
npm run lint, build, test:smoke green
verification: Manual against :8001 (with back feature 21 deployed): take a needs-approval reel, edit instagram description, Approve & Publish, verify in GHL that the post uses the override and not the global template

features.name: agency_music_upload
features.title: /music Library: multipart file input instead of pasting object_key
features.description: Today MusicLibrary.jsx asks the user for three things: display_name, object_key (free text, e.g. 'agencies/ckp/music/sunset-drive.mp3') and duration_seconds. The user must have uploaded the .mp3 to the back's object storage through another path and paste the key — broken flow. Backend feature 22 adds POST /v1/admin/agencies/{id}/music/upload (multipart). This feature replaces the current form with a file input/dropzone, removes the object_key and duration_seconds fields from the UI (both derived server-side), keeps display_name + is_default, and connects the submit to the new endpoint with FormData.
acceptance: src/features/music/MusicLibrary.jsx form: <input type='file' accept='audio/mpeg,audio/mp4,audio/wav,audio/x-wav'> required on create, label 'Audio file (mp3/m4a/wav, max 20MB)', display_name required, is_default checkbox. object_key and duration_seconds (numeric input) fields removed from the UI.

src/features/music/api.js: new uploadTrack(agencyId, formData) that calls apiRequest('/v1/admin/agencies/{id}/music/upload', {method:'POST', body: FormData}). Keep listTracks, inspectTrack, reconfigureTrack, decommissionTrack. Remove registerTrack from the export (the metadata-only endpoint is gone).
src/features/music/hooks.js: useUploadTrack replaces useRegisterTrack; useReconfigureTrack/useDecommissionTrack/useTracks intact.
The form shows progress feedback (while the upload is in progress, disable submit and show 'Uploading…').

Error handling: 400 invalid MIME → 'Unsupported format. Use mp3, m4a or wav.'; 413 → 'File too large (max 20MB)'; 400 MUSIC_TRACK_AUDIO_INVALID → 'Could not process audio (corrupt file?)'; other 4xx → backend message.

The list after a successful upload shows the new track with duration_seconds derived from the file (not from user input) and object_key as an opaque identifier (not editable, optional: show it in small monospace).
The editor (edit mode) for an existing track only allows changing display_name and is_default. The object_key/duration_seconds field appears as read-only (grayed).
Playwright test (tests/playwright/music_upload.spec.js): upload a small .mp3 fixture, verify it appears in the list with duration_seconds > 0.
grep -rn 'object_key' src/features/music returns hits only for reading (display/compare), not for writing from the form.
grep -rn 'fetch(' src/features/music returns 0 hits.
npm run lint finishes green
npm run build finishes green
npm run test:smoke finishes green
verification: Manual against :8001 (with back feature 22 deployed): upload an .mp3, check the track appears with correct duration and plays via the .../file/{filename} endpoint if preview is implemented

features.name: wire_render_to_agency_music_tracks_noop_front
features.title: (noop front) wire_render_to_agency_music_tracks — no required changes in the front
features.description: Symbolic mirror of backend feature 23 to preserve the same-id cross-repo pattern. The backend reorients the renderer to read agency_music_tracks instead of assets/music/ and seeds each agency with the 4 NCS tracks. The frontend needs no changes: the /music screen already shows the active agency's tracks via /v1/admin/agencies/{id}/music. This entry exists only so the front's feature_list.json documents that 23 was back-only.
acceptance: After deploying backend feature 23, the /music tab keeps listing agency tracks normally (including the 4 seeded NCS tracks).
Uploading a reel post-deploy generates an MP4 that uses one of the listed tracks (manual verification, since the frontend has no direct visibility into the filter graph).
This entry is closed immediately as done once the manual behavior is confirmed, with no code commits.
verification: Manual against :8001 (with back feature 23 deployed): check that /music lists 4 NCS tracks for a preexisting agency and that ingesting a property produces a reel that uses one of them

features.name: agency_music_selection_rules
features.title: /music Rules: connect the fallback_to_full_library Toggle to GET/PUT /defaults
features.description: MusicRules.jsx has a 'Fall back to full library if no default track exists' Toggle with onChange={() => {}} — decorative. Backend feature 24 persists the rule in agency_reel_defaults.settings.music.selection_rules.fallback_to_full_library. This feature hydrates the Toggle from GET /defaults and persists changes with PUT /defaults preserving the rest of the settings blob.
acceptance: src/features/music/MusicRules.jsx Toggle connected: reads fallback_to_full_library from defaults.settings.music.selection_rules (default true if absent) and persists with PUT /defaults preserving the rest of the settings blob.
Loading state while GET /defaults is in flight; error state if the PUT fails.
If GET /defaults returns a blob without music.selection_rules, the Toggle shows true (default) without writing anything until the user explicitly changes it.
Reuse the existing useDefaults/useUpdateDefaults; DO NOT duplicate direct fetch to the endpoint.
Playwright test (tests/playwright/music_rules.spec.js): toggle off → PUT body contains settings.music.selection_rules.fallback_to_full_library=false; reload keeps state.
If the /music page is without selected agency the Toggle stays disabled (parity with Library).
npm run lint, build, test:smoke green
verification: Manual against :8001 (with back feature 24 deployed): toggle off, refresh and check persistence; ingest property in agency with empty default pool and toggle off → backend marks reel as failed with code MUSIC_NO_DEFAULT_TRACKS

features.name: per_reel_music_override
features.title: /reels: per-reel track selector (override) — PATCH /v1/admin/reels/{id}/music
features.description: The dead music:'' field in src/features/reels/hooks.js:101 is a vestige of the retired mock. Backend feature 25 adds reels.music_id nullable + PATCH /v1/admin/reels/{reel_id}/music. This feature adds a track selector in the /reels card/table (and/or in the ReelEditor) with the agency tracks + 'Agency default pool' option. On change fires the PATCH and re-enqueues the render. If publish_status is not needs-approval/pending_review, the selector appears disabled with an explanatory tooltip (parity with feature 21 descriptions override).
acceptance: src/features/reels/ReelCard.jsx (or ReelsTable.jsx, UX decision): 'Music' dropdown with 'Agency default pool' option + list of agency tracks (useTracks reused from features/music).
Changing the dropdown fires PATCH /v1/admin/reels/{reel_id}/music with {music_id: <id|null>}; UI shows a small spinner during the request and 'Re-rendering with new track…' message on success.
If publish_status not in ['needs-approval','pending_review'] the dropdown shows disabled with tooltip 'Track cannot be changed after approving/publishing'.
Error handling: 409 RESOURCE_LOCKED → explanatory banner; 403/404 cross-agency → 'Track not available'; others → back message.
The dead music:'' field in hooks.js:101 is replaced by music_id and a denormalized music {display_name} that the API returns via include or a one-off fetch; remove all traces of the mock.
Playwright test: open a needs-approval reel, change the track to a specific one → the PATCH arrives with correct music_id; change to 'Agency default pool' → PATCH with music_id=null.
Playwright test: approved reel → dropdown disabled.
grep -rn "music: ''" src returns 0 hits after the feature.
npm run lint, build, test:smoke green

features.name: review_emails_chip_editor
features.title: /automation: multi-email chip editor for reviewEmails (replaces the plain CSV input)
features.description: Today `reviewEmails` in `/automation` is a plain CSV `<input>` in ReviewModeDetails.jsx:22 without validation. Back feature 26 prepares the email infrastructure and feature 27 wires the actual sending. This feature replaces the plain input with an `EmailListInput` (chips in the style of feature 20's HashtagsEditor — based on `SocialConfig.jsx:466-566`): commit with Enter/comma/space, normalization (trim+lowercase), silent dedup, validation with regex `EMAIL_PATTERN` (`^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$`), inline danger-banner error. State goes from CSV string to `list[str]`; `useAutomationSave.js` sends the array directly to `defaults.settings.automation.reviewEmails` (the back accepts both shapes for compat). The mock-backend extends its validation to reject invalid shapes (e.g. number, dict) with 422 while accepting legacy CSV string and list[str].
features.depends_on: 4Reels-Backend feature 26 (email infrastructure) + feature 27 (review_requested subscriber) deployed on :8001
acceptance: src/lib/utils/email.js exports EMAIL_PATTERN (regex), isValidEmail(value:string)→bool, normaliseEmail(raw:string)→string (trim + lowercase).

src/features/automation/EmailListInput.jsx (or analogous component) renders chips with × to delete; input with commit on Enter/comma/space/blur; Backspace-deletes-last if input is empty; regex validation on add + dedup; inline error on rejection.
src/features/automation/ReviewModeDetails.jsx uses EmailListInput instead of the current plain <input> (line 22).

src/features/automation/AutomationConfig.jsx state changes to list<string> instead of CSV string; hydration from defaults.settings: if legacy CSV arrives, split by comma + trim + filter non-empty; if array, use it directly.
src/features/automation/useAutomationSave.js sends settings.automation.reviewEmails as an array of strings directly (back accepts).
src/features/defaults/initialState.js: AUTOMATION_SETTINGS_KEYS.reviewEmails default value changes to [] (empty array) instead of '' (empty string).
tests/support/mock-backend.js /defaults handler: accepts settings.automation.reviewEmails as list[str] or legacy CSV string; rejects invalid types with 422 INVALID_EMAIL_LIST.

Playwright test tests/review_emails.spec.js: navigate /automation → review mode → add 3 emails (one invalid is discarded inline) → Save → PUT body contains array of 2 normalized emails; reload keeps chips.
grep -rn 'reviewEmails.*split' src returns only the legacy hydration branch; split is not used in the save.
npm run lint, build, test:smoke green.
verification: Manual against :8001 (with back 26+27 deployed): configure 2 reviewEmails, ingest property with approval_required, wait for pending_review, verify both emails arrive with link to the reel editor.

features.name: brand_dynamic_fonts_and_reset_defaults
features.title: /brand: fonts dropdown populated by GET /fonts + 'Reset to default' button on colors and font
features.description: The font dropdown in BrandConfig.jsx today is a hardcoded array of 5 (Inter/Söhne/Manrope/Plus Jakarta Sans/Helvetica). Söhne and Helvetica are fictional (not on disk), Inter is the only real one, and font_family is a dead-end (back does not use it). Back feature 28 brings Manrope+Plus Jakarta Sans+Montserrat+Poppins+Roboto under assets/fonts/, exposes GET /v1/admin/fonts and wires font_family to the renderer. This feature: (1) replaces the hardcoded FONTS array with a GET /fonts call via new `src/features/brand/fontsApi.js` + `useAvailableFonts` hook. (2) Adds a 'Reset to default' button (↺ icon or text) next to each selector (primary_color, secondary_color, font_family) that sends `null` in the PUT, returning the field to the webhook/global fallback. (3) Visual UI hint 'Using default (webhook fallback)' when the field is null after the GET. (4) Mock-backend extends /fonts handler.
acceptance: src/features/brand/fontsApi.js + useAvailableFonts() hook: listFonts() calls GET /v1/admin/fonts, cache during the session, returns {items, loading, error}.
src/features/brand/BrandConfig.jsx: font_family dropdown populates from useAvailableFonts(); includes option 'Default (system fallback)' value='' (serialized to null in the PUT body).

src/features/brand/BrandConfig.jsx: next to each ColorInput (primary, secondary) and to the font dropdown, a 'Reset to default' button (with tooltip 'Use webhook color' or 'Use system font') that sets state to null and shows placeholder 'Using default'.
buildBrandBody: if state.primaryColor === null → body.primary_color = null (the key is not omitted). Same for secondary_color and font_family.
If GET /brand returns null fields, the UI shows them as 'Using default' (with the real value rendered in a future preview if any).

tests/support/mock-backend.js: GET /v1/admin/fonts handler returns 6 items with correct shape; PUT /brand accepts null in the 3 fields; PUT with font_family outside the catalog → 422 UNKNOWN_FONT_FAMILY.
tests/support/mock-backend.js: the hardcoded FONTS in the front disappears (grep 'Söhne\|Plus Jakarta Sans' src returns 0 hits in BrandConfig.jsx).

Playwright test tests/brand_dynamic_fonts.spec.js: load /brand → dropdown populates via GET /fonts; select Manrope → PUT with font_family='Manrope' → 200; click 'Reset to default' on font → PUT with font_family=null → 200; same for colors.
npm run lint, build, test:smoke green.
verification: Manual against :8001 (with back feature 28 deployed): /brand shows 6 fonts in dropdown; select Manrope → save → verify in DB that brand.font_family='Manrope'; click reset → null in DB.

features.name: social_per_platform_publish_toggle
features.title: /social: per-platform toggle to enable/disable publishing, persisted in defaults.platforms
features.description: Today the /social frontend shows a `ConnectionsStrip` with the icons of the connected networks (read-only, no control). The backend has `agency_reel_defaults.platforms` (JSONB array) that controls which networks the pipeline publishes to on ingest — the list is managed from `/defaults` (existing selector) and by default includes the 7 platforms after feature 19. This feature adds in `/social` a toggle per network ('Publish on this network') that enables/disables the corresponding entry in `defaults.platforms` and persists via the existing endpoint `PUT /v1/admin/agencies/{id}/defaults`. The back requires NO changes — only the existing field is reused. UX: next to each network chip (in ConnectionsStrip or in a dedicated section), a Switch toggle that paints green when inside the array. If the network is not connected (no social account), the toggle appears disabled with a tooltip 'Connect this network first'. The templates tab of a disabled network remains editable (so the user can prepare templates) but with a 'Disabled' visual badge.
features.depends_on: None (backend feature 6 and feature 19 already delivered the platforms field and the PUT /defaults endpoint).
acceptance: src/features/social/SocialConfig.jsx reads `defaults.platforms` (via useReelDefaults or defaultsApi.getDefaults) and derives enabled/disabled state per network.
Each network in the strip list (or in a dedicated section of the page) has a 'Publish' toggle reflecting whether the network is in the array.

Toggle ON → network present in the array; OFF → network absent. The change is persisted via PUT /v1/admin/agencies/{id}/defaults with the updated `platforms` array (preserving the rest of the defaults payload fields).
If the network is NOT connected (useSocials().connected === false), the toggle appears disabled with an explanatory tooltip.
Templates subtabs of disabled networks appear faded (CSS class `disabled` or `off`) but remain clickable to edit templates.
The change is reflected immediately in the UI (optimistic update); if the PUT fails, rollback + error banner.
tests/support/mock-backend.js: the PUT /defaults handler already accepts the platforms field; verify that the round-trip works and that the GET response reflects the new array.

Playwright test tests/social_publish_toggles.spec.js: (a) toggle off of a connected network → PUT body contains `platforms` without that network; (b) toggle on of a previously disabled network → PUT body contains the network; (c) reload keeps state; (d) toggle of a non-connected network → disabled.
If `defaults.platforms` arrives null/absent, assume the back's default array (7 platforms after feature 19).
npm run lint, build, test:smoke green.
verification: Manual against :8001: in /social disable 'tiktok' → verify via GET /defaults that platforms does not contain 'tiktok'; ingest property → reel is not published to TikTok.

features.name: subtitles_tab_cleanup_and_autocaptions_switch
features.title: /defaults Subtitles: autoCaptions switch, remove Karaoke, remove LivePreview
features.description: Backend feature 31 wires all subtitle settings to the renderer. This feature cleans up the UI: (1) Adds an 'Auto-generate AI subtitles' switch at the top of SubtitlesTab (reflects defaults.settings['automation.autoCaptions']); when off, the other controls dim but remain editable (to prepare config for when re-enabled). (2) Removes the 'Word highlight' (karaoke) card — removing the subHighlightWord and subHighlightColor inputs from SubtitlesTab + initialState. (3) Removes the <LivePreview /> invocation from ReelDefaultsConfig.jsx; the LivePreview.jsx component may remain as deadcode (next housekeeping removes it) or be removed in this same PR (implementer's call).
features.depends_on: 4Reels-Backend feature 31 (subtitle_settings_wiring) deployed on :8001 so the renderer applies the settings.
acceptance: src/features/defaults/tabs/SubtitlesTab.jsx has an 'Auto-generate AI subtitles' Toggle at the top that writes to state['automation.autoCaptions'].
When autoCaptions = false, the Typography and Background&position cards appear dimmed (class 'subdued' or 'off') with a hint 'AI subtitles are disabled — these settings will apply when re-enabled'.
The 'Word highlight' card (subHighlightWord + subHighlightColor) is removed entirely from SubtitlesTab.jsx.
src/features/defaults/initialState.js: subHighlightWord and subHighlightColor keys removed.
src/features/defaults/ReelDefaultsConfig.jsx: <LivePreview /> is no longer invoked. The /defaults page shows only sidenav + content without the preview column.
src/features/defaults/defaults.css: media queries and rules related to .defaults-content/.defaults-preview adjusted (if orphaned).
grep -rn 'subHighlightWord\|subHighlightColor\|LivePreview' src returns 0 hits (or only in LivePreview.jsx if marked deadcode is kept).

Smoke Playwright test (may be an extension of existing /defaults tests): toggle autoCaptions → PUT contains automation.autoCaptions=false; reload keeps state; Subtitles tab content no longer has 'Word highlight' or 'Karaoke'.
npm run lint, build, test:smoke green.
verification: Manual against :8001 (with back feature 31 deployed): toggle autoCaptions off + Save → ingest property → the reel is rendered without subtitles in the ffmpeg drawtext.

features.name: reels_list_pagination_and_filters_ui
features.title: /reels: pagination (page+pageSize+total) and basic filters (workflow_state, publish_status, search)
features.description: Today Dashboard calls reelsApi.list(agencyId) without pagination: the back returns up to limit=50 without offset or real total. This feature wires up the back's real pagination (4Reels-Backend feature 32 cross-repo) and adds filters + search. (1) reelsApi.list accepts {page, pageSize, workflowState, publishStatus, q} and sends them as query params; consumes {items, count_total, page, page_size, has_more}. (2) Dashboard.jsx + ReelsTable.jsx show page_size selector (10/25/50, default 25), ‹ › navigation with 'Showing A–B of N', dropdown filters for workflow_state and publish_status, search input debounced 300ms over title/slug. (3) Pagination state in URL (?page=2&page_size=25&workflow_state=needs_approval&q=cranford) using the existing tab router pattern; sharing a link reproduces the same state. (4) Changing filter or page_size resets page to 1. (5) Loading skeleton only in the table, without flicker in header or metrics.
acceptance: src/features/reels/api.js: reelsApi.list({agencyId, page, pageSize, workflowState, publishStatus, q}) builds query params correctly and only sends the ones that are defined.
src/features/reels/hooks.js: useReels accepts filters + pagination and exposes {items, countTotal, page, pageSize, hasMore, loading, refresh}.
Dashboard.jsx shows pagination controls: '‹ Showing A–B of N ›' + page_size dropdown (10/25/50), dropdown filters and search input.
Change page → new request to back; change filter or pageSize → resets page=1.
Pagination + filters state persists in URL search params; pasting a link with ?page=3&q=cranford reproduces the same state on load.
Search input debounced 300ms (no request spam per keystroke).
When count_total=0 shows empty state distinct from loading state.
Playwright tests: navigation page 1 → 2 updates table; workflow_state filter filters; q search filters; URL reflects state.
npm run lint, build, test:smoke green.
verification: Manual against :8001 with back feature 32 deployed: load /reels, paginate, filter by workflow_state=needs_approval, search 'cranford', reload with ?page=2 in URL and verify it is respected.

features.name: agency_outro_upload_ui
features.title: /defaults Intro & Outro: wire real OUTRO upload to the backend
features.description: Replaces the local state of the Outro card in IntroOutroCard.jsx with real backend calls (4Reels-Backend feature 33). (1) src/features/defaults/api.js exposes outroUpload(file), outroDelete(), outroFileUrl(agencyId) using the pattern of feature 9 logo upload (FormData multipart, no JSON Content-Type). (2) IntroOutroCard.jsx in 'Outro' mode shows progress bar during upload, error states (size/duration/mime), and the 'replace/trash' chip wired to DELETE. (3) 'Enabled' toggle persists outro_enabled in defaults via the existing PUT. (4) Source selector: 'Uploaded video' enabled; 'Brand card' and 'None' present but 'Brand card' disabled with tooltip 'Coming soon'. (5) Client validation: size ≤50MB, mime video/mp4|quicktime, duration ≤10s via local probe with <video> + onloadedmetadata. (6) When outro_source!='uploaded' the preview shows a placeholder consistent with the existing Brand/None card.
acceptance: src/features/defaults/api.js: outroUpload, outroDelete, outroFileUrl aligned with feature 9 (logo upload) and feature 22 (music upload). No VITE_ADMIN_API_TOKEN nor secrets in bundle.
IntroOutroCard.jsx in 'Outro' mode uses real multipart upload; file chip reflects size + duration from the backend; replace and trash work.
Enabled toggle of the Outro card persists outro_enabled in defaults (existing defaults endpoint PUT).
Source segmented: 'Uploaded video' enabled; 'Brand card' disabled + tooltip 'Coming soon'; 'None' enabled and clears outro_object_key via DELETE.
Client validation blocks files >50MB, non-video mime, duration >10s with inline error (does not crash or send the upload).
Playwright tests: upload a short MP4 → see chip; toggle off+on preserves file; trash fires DELETE.
npm run lint, build, test:smoke green.
verification: Manual against :8001 with back feature 33: upload 5s MP4, see preview; toggle off+save+on+save; trash; ingest property with outro_enabled → see outro at the end of the rendered reel.

features.name: agency_intro_upload_ui
features.title: /defaults Intro & Outro: wire real INTRO upload to the backend
features.description: Symmetric to feature 33 but for the Intro card. Reuses the same multipart client (introUpload, introDelete, introFileUrl) and the same UX. Implementer may factor an UploadVideoCard component if they find significant duplication with outro; reviewer's call. The only functional difference with feature 33 is the copy ('Plays at the start of every reel' vs 'Plays at the end') and the endpoints (/intro/... instead of /outro/...).

acceptance: src/features/defaults/api.js: introUpload, introDelete, introFileUrl symmetric to feature 33.
IntroOutroCard.jsx in 'Intro' mode uses real multipart upload with the same pattern as outro.
Enabled toggle of the Intro card persists intro_enabled in defaults.
Source segmented same as feature 33 (Uploaded enabled, Brand card disabled, None enabled).
Client validation same as feature 33.
Playwright tests similar to feature 33 but for intro.
npm run lint, build, test:smoke green.

features.name: per_reel_photos_override_ui
features.title: /reels editor: persist reorder and photo selection (PATCH photos)
features.description: Today PhotosPanel.jsx in the editor only modifies local React state: reorder with setPhotos and toggle selected with setPhotos. Does not persist. This feature wires those changes to the endpoint PATCH /v1/admin/agencies/{id}/reels/{site}/{prop}/photos (4Reels-Backend feature 35). (1) reelsApi.patchReelPhotos(agencyId, siteId, sourcePropertyId, [{position, selected}, ...]). (2) PhotosPanel: the final drop of the reorder and click-to-toggle fire PATCH (debounce 500ms to batch several moves). (3) Optimistic UI: local state changes immediately; on fail rollback + toast. (4) After a successful PATCH, the render is re-enqueued in the back and the back returns render_status='pending' on the next read of the reel; UI shows 'Re-rendering...' badge until it returns to 'done'. (5) If the reel is in workflow_state=approved or published, the PATCH returns 409 and the UI shows error 'Cannot edit a reel that has already been approved' (same pattern as feature 25 music).
acceptance: src/features/reels/api.js: patchReelPhotos consumes {photos: [{position, selected}, ...]}; pydantic extra=forbid on back enforces exact body.
src/features/reels/editor/PhotosPanel.jsx: reorder fires PATCH with debounce 500ms; toggle selected fires PATCH with debounce 500ms; several changes within <500ms are batched in a single request.
Optimistic UI with rollback on fail; error toast with back message.
After successful PATCH, UI reads render_status and shows 'Re-rendering...' badge until done.
409 (reel approved/published) is shown as a persistent banner in the editor with copy 'Cannot edit a reel that has already been approved'.
Playwright tests: drop-reorder fires PATCH; toggle fires PATCH; 409 renders correctly.
npm run lint, build, test:smoke green.
verification: Manual against :8001 with back feature 35: open editor of a needs_approval reel, reorder photos, see render_status='pending', wait for 'done', verify the rendered reel reflects the new order.

features.name: per_reel_subtitles_override_ui
features.title: /reels editor: persist subtitle edits (text and timings) via PATCH subtitles
features.description: Today SubtitlesPanel.jsx edits in local state with setSubtitles (text, in/out time). This feature wires up the PATCH /v1/admin/agencies/{id}/reels/{site}/{prop}/subtitles (4Reels-Backend feature 36). (1) reelsApi.patchReelSubtitles(agencyId, siteId, sourcePropertyId, [{index, text, in_seconds, out_seconds}, ...]). (2) SubtitlesPanel: each edit (text, in, out, add, delete) marks dirty; auto-save debounced 1s. (3) Strict client validation (mirrors back): in_seconds>=0, out_seconds>in_seconds, no overlap, text 1..200, indices unique and monotonic; on violation → inline red error + no PATCH. (4) Optimistic UI + rollback on fail. (5) After successful PATCH, render is re-enqueued and 'Re-rendering...' badge until done. (6) 409 if reel approved/published, same pattern as feature 35.
acceptance: src/features/reels/api.js: patchReelSubtitles consumes {cues: [{index, text, in_seconds, out_seconds}, ...]}.
src/features/reels/editor/SubtitlesPanel.jsx: edits fire PATCH (debounce 1s or explicit save per implementer); client validation blocks in>=out and shows overlap warning.
Optimistic UI with rollback on fail.
After successful PATCH, 'Re-rendering...' badge until render_status='done'.
409 shown as banner in the editor.
Playwright tests: editing a cue's text fires PATCH; moving timings fires PATCH; 409 renders.
npm run lint, build, test:smoke green.
verification: Manual against :8001 with back feature 36: open editor, change a cue's text, wait for PATCH, see render_status='pending' → 'done', verify the rendered reel shows the new text.

features.name: per_reel_slides_override_ui
features.title: /reels editor: persist slides manifest edits (scene order and durations)
features.description: Today SlidesPanel.jsx / SlideRow allow editing the scene list (not only photos: also voiceover scenes, intro/outro scenes). Changes in local state. This feature wires up the PATCH /v1/admin/agencies/{id}/reels/{site}/{prop}/slides (4Reels-Backend feature 37). (1) reelsApi.patchReelSlides(agencyId, siteId, sourcePropertyId, [{slide_id, position, duration_seconds, kind, ...}, ...]). (2) SlidesPanel: scene reorder, duration edit, toggle visibility/skip fire PATCH (debounce 500ms). (3) Client validation: sum of durations <= target_duration_seconds (from defaults); warning if exceeded but persist allowed (back decides). (4) Optimistic + rollback. (5) Render re-enqueued, 'Re-rendering...' badge. (6) 409 if published.
features.depends_on: 4Reels-Backend feature 37 (per_reel_slides_override) deployed on :8001. After features 35 and 36 to reuse common utilities (render-status badge, 409 banner, optimistic helper) that the previous ones may have factored.
acceptance: src/features/reels/api.js: patchReelSlides consumes the manifest shape agreed with back feature 37.
SlidesPanel/SlideRow: reorder and edit fire PATCH with debounce 500ms.
Client validation warns if sum of durations exceeds target_duration; allows continuing (user decision).
Optimistic + rollback; 'Re-rendering...' badge until done; 409 banner if reel published.
Playwright tests: slide reorder fires PATCH; duration edit fires PATCH.
npm run lint, build, test:smoke green.
verification: Manual against :8001 with back feature 37: reorder slides, wait for 'Re-rendering...', verify the reel reflects the new scene order.

features.name: live_state_sync_reels_dashboard_and_editor
features.title: Global Toaster + Dashboard refreshes after closing editor with mutations (the modified reel rises to the top without reloading)
features.description: Two combined problems the user perceives as one: (1) the /reels Dashboard is not invalidated after mutations made inside the editor overlay (photos, music, subtitles, slides, descriptions, approve, reject from the editor), so the modified reel does NOT rise to the top even though the backend already orders correctly by updated_at DESC. (2) Dashboard actions like approve/reject (Dashboard.jsx:135-149) and Brand save give no feedback to the user — silent refetch and inline card that is lost. This feature adds: (a) global toast system (`<Toaster>` mounted in Shell.jsx + useToast hook with success/error/info); (b) mechanism for the editor to tell the Dashboard when it closed after significant mutations → Dashboard does automatic refetch() and the modified reel appears at the top instantly. Scope bounded to /reels (Dashboard + editor) per user decision; Brand/Music/Templates out of scope.

front: Create src/lib/hooks/useToast.js: implements a singleton module with a toast queue and React useToast() hook exposing {success(msg, opts), error(msg, opts), info(msg, opts), dismiss(id)}. Uses Context+Provider (`ToastProvider`) mounted in Shell.jsx above TenantProvider so any feature can call it. Each toast auto-dismisses after 4s (success/info) or 6s (error). Opts: { duration?: ms, id?: string for dedupe }. No external library.

Create src/shared/Toaster.jsx: component that renders the queue in a fixed position (top-right or bottom-center, implementer's call aligned with the current design system — look at other components in src/shared/). Each toast is accessible (role='alert', aria-live='polite' for info/success, 'assertive' for error).
Mount <ToastProvider><Toaster/>...</ToastProvider> in Shell.jsx wrapping the rest of the app.

src/features/reels/Dashboard.jsx: in handleApprove (line ~135) and handleReject (line ~143), wrap the await in try/catch and fire useToast().success('Reel approved') / .error(err.message). Keep the existing refetch(). Temporarily disable the clicked button while in-flight (use useMutation loading state).

src/features/reels/editor/ReelEditor.jsx: add 'hasMutated' tracking (useState false). Each child panel (Photos, Music, Subtitles, Slides, Descriptions) that already fires a successful mutation must propagate via onMutate prop → setHasMutated(true). When the editor closes (onClose handler or navigation back to /reels), if hasMutated and there is an onAfterEditorClose callback from the parent Dashboard, call it.

src/features/reels/ReelsRoute (or the component that mounts Dashboard + Outlet in Shell.jsx): pass to the editor a callback that calls the Dashboard's useReels() refetch(). Pattern: the Dashboard exposes its refetch via a simple Context `DashboardRefetchContext` that the editor consumes with useContext.

Fire a toast on every editor mutation (photos/music/subs/slides/descriptions) in addition to the existing inline feedback — the inline feedback may stay as contextual confirmation (e.g.: 'Re-rendering with new photo order…'); the toast gives global confirmation (e.g.: 'Photo order saved').

Playwright E2E test: tests/reels_dashboard_live_sync.spec.js (new file) — opens Dashboard, opens a reel in position ≥2, fires a mutation (music override or similar), closes the editor, verifies the reel is now in position 1 without reload. Mock backend in tests/support/mock-backend.js must support the flow.
Feedback E2E test: add asserts in Dashboard spec verifying that after clicking Approve a toast with expected text appears.
No changes to lib/api/client.js or to the backend.

acceptance: Global `<Toaster>` exists mounted in Shell.jsx and a reusable useToast() hook.
Approve/Reject from Dashboard fires success or error toast.
Mutation inside the editor (photos/music/subs/slides override) fires success or error toast in addition to the existing inline feedback.
Closing the editor after at least one mutation fires Dashboard's refetch() automatically; the modified reel appears in the first position (updated_at DESC order already guaranteed by the backend).
Closing the editor without having mutated anything does NOT fire an unnecessary refetch.
New E2E tests (live-sync + toast) green; existing suite without regression (with the known flaky social_templates tablet documented as pre-existing).
npm run build + npm run lint green.
Harness init (bash ./init.sh) exit 0.
verification: Manual against :8001: open /reels, identify a reel at position 5+, change its music, close editor, verify it rises to the first row without reload and a success toast appears.
Manual: approve a reel from Dashboard, verify success toast and that it leaves the 'Needs approval' tab.
features.leader_notes: Cross-repo feature paired with backend #39 (test_reels_list_ordering_guard). Repo rules: do NOT add TypeScript, React Query, Tailwind, MSW, styled-components, @emotion. Vanilla React 18 + Vite + Playwright. The toast must use the primitives already in src/shared/ and respect the design system (look at BrandConfig.jsx → card .info/.success/.danger as visual reference). The change must NOT break existing E2E (321 passing baseline + 1 flaky tablet of social_templates that is pre-existing and not addressed here).

features.name: manual_reel_regenerate_button
features.title: /reels editor: 'Render again' button that fires a manual re-render without touching workflow
features.description: Adds a visible button in the ReelEditor (header or separate panel) that fires POST /regenerate (back feature 40). Visible only when render_status='completed' AND publish_status!='published'. Click → confirm modal → POST without body → immediate Re-rendering badge + render_status poll. Specific 409 codes surfaced as toast.
acceptance: reelsApi.regenerateReel(agencyId,siteId,sourcePropertyId) POST without body to feature 40 endpoint.
'Render again' button visible in the ReelEditor header when render_status=='completed' and publish_status!='published'.
Click → confirm modal 'This will re-render the reel using current settings. Continue?'.
After confirm: POST fired, 'Re-rendering…' badge immediate (reuses RerenderBadge from lockedReelHelpers), render_status poll (reuses primitive).
409 REGENERATE_PUBLISHED_FORBIDDEN → toast 'Cannot re-render a published reel'.
409 REGENERATE_ALREADY_IN_FLIGHT → toast 'A render is already in progress for this reel'.
Button hidden or disabled (with explanatory tooltip) when publish_status=='published'.
Playwright tests: happy path; 409 published path; 409 in_flight path.
npm run lint, build, test:smoke green.
verification: Manual against :8001: open editor of a completed reel, click 'Render again', confirm, see badge, wait ~2min and verify new render.
